---
title: "Creating Coordinate Systems From CSV"
description: "Inputting custom coordinate systems automatically"
date: "2026-03-03"
---

[Home](/)


# ANSYS Mechanical Automation: Coordinate Systems From CSV

Often times clients will provide CSV files with information; in this case, I was given coordinate a table with coordinate systems' locations and their names.

To accomplish this, I went through by row (ignoring the first), and edited the coordinate system object per iteration:
```python
import csv

cs = Model.CoordinateSystems

file = '<PATH>'

with open(file, 'r') as csvF:
    reader = csv.reader(csvF)
    next(reader)
    
    for x in reader:
        newCS = cs.AddCoordinateSystem()
        newCS.Name = r"""""" + x[0] 
        newCS.OriginDefineBy = CoordinateSystemAlignmentType.Fixed
        
        newCS.OriginX = Quantity(float(x[1]), "m")
        newCS.OriginY = Quantity(float(x[2]), "m")
        newCS.OriginZ = Quantity(float(x[3]), "m")
```
